name: freepaths

metadata.name: freepaths
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License (GPL)

documentation:
# FreePATHS - Free Phonon And Thermal Simulator

This Monte Carlo algorithm simulates trajectories of phonons in 3D models of nanostructures, which consists of a box with holes or pillars of various shapes. The algorithm outputs phonon trajectories, heat fluxes, temperature maps and profiles, the thermal conductivity, scattering maps and statistics and other information. See [documentation](https://anufrievroman.gitbook.io/freepaths/) for the details of the simulation.

![Screenshot](https://github.com/anufrievroman/Monte-Carlo/blob/master/screenshot.png)


## Installation

[Installation is detailed in documentation](https://anufrievroman.gitbook.io/freepaths/installation). In short, install the package from PyPi repository by entering this command into a terminal or a python console:

`pip install --upgrade freepaths`


## Usage

FreePATHS is a command line application, so it runs inside Linux, MacOS, or Windows terminal. It takes an input file from the user, which contains all the settings, and outputs the results in a new folder.

There are two modes of using the program. Main mode traces a large number of phonons through a structure and collects statistics about their paths. The MFP sampling mode measures phonon mean free paths using a small number of phonons and calculates the thermal conductivity by integrating phonon dispersion. 

### Demo

If you simply run `freepaths` without specifying an input file, the program will run a demo simulation.

### Main mode

In the main mode, the program traces large number of phonons through a structure and calculates various statistical distributions and maps. In this mode, the thermal conductivity will be calculated via Fourier law. Note that the thermal conductivity will be correct only in the absence of holes.

Run the program as:

`freepaths your_input_file.py`

See [documentation](https://anufrievroman.gitbook.io/freepaths/examples) for explanations about creating your own input files. In the [examples](https://github.com/anufrievroman/freepaths/tree/master/examples) folder, you will find examples of various input files. Try using one of them, for instance as:

`freepaths simple_nanowire.py`

After the simulation, see the results in a newly created **Results** folder.


### MFP sampling mode

Alternatively, you can run FreePATHS in the mean free path sampling mode, which is designed to calculate the thermal conductivity by integrating phonon dispersion. To run the program in this mode, it is advised to reduce the number of phonons to about 30 and add `-s` flag in the command:

`freepaths -s simple_nanowire.py`

The calculated thermal conductivity will be output in the terminal. However, other statistical quantities and plots will still be calculated and output in the `Results` folder.


## Troubleshooting

- [Troubles with installation](https://anufrievroman.gitbook.io/freepaths/installation)
- [Troubles with usage](https://anufrievroman.gitbook.io/freepaths/usage)


## Disclaimer

The code is still in development and provided as is. It likely contains bugs or might be inappropriate for your research. It is your responsibility to understand the underlying physics, test the code, and verify that the equations and the code are correct. See [documentation](https://anufrievroman.gitbook.io/freepaths) and the references below for more details on the code. Please use only [officially published releases](https://anufrievroman.gitbook.io/freepaths/installation) of the code and not current main branch, which can be unstable.


## References and acknowledgments

The code is developed by [Roman Anufriev](https://anufrievroman.com), Philipp Gassmann, and other contributors in [Nomura lab](https://www.nlab.iis.u-tokyo.ac.jp/index-e.html) at the University of Tokyo since 2018.
If you would like to use this code for your research, please see the disclaimer above and consider citing the papers below, if it is appropriate.
Details of the code and examples of the output can be found in the following papers:

1. Anufriev et al. [Materials Today Physics 15, 100272 (2021)](https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S2542529320300961)
2. Anufriev et al. [Nanoscale, 11, 13407-13414 (2019)](https://pubs.rsc.org/en/content/articlehtml/2019/nr/c9nr03863a)
3. Anufriev et al. [ACS Nano 12, 11928 (2018)](https://pubs.acs.org/doi/abs/10.1021/acsnano.8b07597)
4. Huang et al. [ACS Applied Materials & Interfaces 12, 25478 (2020)](https://pubs.acs.org/doi/10.1021/acsami.0c06030)

Development of this code was funded by the following grants:

- PRESTO JST (No. JPMJPR19I1)
- CREST JST (No. JPMJCR19Q3)
- Kakenhi (15H05869, 15K13270, and 18K14078)
- Postdoctoral Fellowship program of Japan Society for the Promotion of Science.

modules.name: freepaths.scattering
documentation:
Modules provides scattering processes on various complex objects.
Each function determines whether the scattering should happen and
call corresponding function for scattering on corresponding primitive.

functions.name: freepaths.scattering.internal_scattering
documentation:
Check if the time passed since previous diffuse scattering event reached
the time until an internal scattering event, and if yes, scatters randomly

functions.name: freepaths.scattering.reinitialization
documentation: Re-thermalize (diffusely) phonon when it comes back to one of the hot sides

functions.name: freepaths.scattering.scattering_on_right_sidewall
documentation: Scatter phonon if it reached right side wall

functions.name: freepaths.scattering.scattering_on_left_sidewall
documentation: Scatter phonon if it reached left side wall

functions.name: freepaths.scattering.scattering_on_top_sidewall
documentation: Check if the phonon hits top side wall and output new vector

functions.name: freepaths.scattering.scattering_on_bottom_sidewall
documentation: Check if the phonon hits bottom side wall and output new vector

functions.name: freepaths.scattering.floor_scattering
documentation: Check if the phonon hits the floor surface and calculate new angles

functions.name: freepaths.scattering.ceiling_scattering
documentation: Check if the phonon hits the ceiling surface and if this place has a pillar and output new vector

functions.name: freepaths.scattering.surface_scattering
documentation: Check for a surface scattering on this step

modules.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives
documentation: Module that provides scattering functions on basic objects like various walls

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.specularity
documentation: Calculate probability of specular scattering with Soffer's equation

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.no_new_scattering
documentation:
Check if new angles do not immediately lead to a new top/bottom or sidewall scattering event.
Such additional scatering event may cause troubles because at this stage we already checked the domain boundaries.
Thus, this check is necessary to prevent phonons leaving the structure boundaries.

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.random_scattering
documentation: Scattering in a random direction

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.vertical_surface_left_scattering
documentation: Scattering from a vertical surface to the left

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.vertical_surface_right_scattering
documentation: Scattering from a vertical surface to the left

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.horizontal_surface_down_scattering
documentation: Scattering from a horizontal surface down

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.horizontal_surface_up_scattering
documentation: Scattering from a horizontal surface up

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.inclined_surfaces_down_scattering
documentation: Scattering from a inclined surfaces pointing down like V

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.inclined_surfaces_up_scattering
documentation: Scattering from a inclined surfaces pointing up like ^

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.in_plane_surface_scattering
documentation: Scattering from the ceiling or floor surfaces downwards

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.circle_outer_scattering
documentation: Scattering from the outer surface of the circle

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.circle_inner_scattering
documentation: Scattering from the inner surface of the circle

functions.name: freepaths.scattering_primitives.circle_inclined_inner_scattering
documentation:
Scattering from the inner inclined surface of the circle.
This is used for pillars with inclide walls.
THIS IS NOT TESTED AND NOT WORKING.

methods.name: freepaths.maps.ScatteringMap.__init__
documentation: Initialize arrays of scattering maps

methods.name: freepaths.maps.ScatteringMap.add_scattering_to_map
documentation: Record the place where a scattering event occurred according to the event type

documentation: Write scattering map into file

methods.name: freepaths.maps.ScatteringMap.dump_data

Map of scattering in the structure

classes.name: freepaths.maps.ThermalMaps

methods.name: freepaths.maps.ThermalMaps.__init__
documentation: Initialize arrays of thermal maps and other parameters

methods.name: freepaths.maps.ThermalMaps.calculate_pixel_volumes
documentation: Calculate a map showing if the pixel contains material (1) or a hole (0)

methods.name: freepaths.maps.ThermalMaps.add_energy_to_maps
documentation:
Register the phonon in the pixel corresponding to its current position at certain timestep
and adds it to thermal maps and profiles.
Note that in case of temperature and heat flux profiles, phonon is registered not at
its current timestep number but at a virtual timestep which is current time + first timestep.
This first time step is unique for each phonon. This is done to simulate more relistic continious heat flow.

methods.name: freepaths.maps.ThermalMaps.calculate_weighted_flux
documentation: Calculate heat flux normalized by the number of phonons in each pixel, except where the number is zero

methods.name: freepaths.maps.ThermalMaps.calculate_heat_flux_modulus
documentation: Calculate heat flux modulus as sqrt(q_x^2 + q_y^2)

methods.name: freepaths.maps.ThermalMaps.calculate_thermal_conductivity
documentation:
Calculate the thermal conductivity for each time interval from heat flux
and temperature profiles accumulated in that interval

methods.name: freepaths.maps.ThermalMaps.write_into_files
documentation: Write thermal maps into files

methods.name: freepaths.maps.ThermalMaps.dump_data
documentation: Return data of a process in the form of a dictionary to be attached to the global data
Maps and profiles of thermal energy in the structure

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_mean_free_path_distribution
documentation: Plot distribution of MFP per phonon

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_velocity_distribution
documentation: Plot distribution of group velocities

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_time_in_segments
documentation: Plot time spent in segments

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_thermal_conductivity
documentation: Plot thermal conductivity against time segment

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_temperature_profile
documentation: Plot profile of temperature for each time segment

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_heat_flux_profile
documentation: Plot profile of heat flux for each time segment

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_thermal_map
documentation: Plot thermal map as color map

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_pixel_volumes
documentation: Plot the pixel volumes as 2D map

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_heat_flux_map
documentation: Plot heat flux map as color map

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_scattering_map
documentation: Plot the map of scattering events

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_trajectories
documentation: Plot the phonon trajectories

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_scattering_statistics
documentation: Calculate and plot rates of different scattering events in each length segment

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_structure
documentation: Plot the structure with all the elements

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_material_properties
documentation: Plot phonon dispersion and display some other material properties

functions.name: freepaths.output_plots.plot_data
documentation: Create plots of various distributions, maps, profiles, and other quantities

modules.name: freepaths.scattering_types
documentation:
Module that provides types of phonon scattering that might occur.
These scattering types are returned on each step
so that higher level modules know what has happened.

documentation: Possible scattering types

methods.name: freepaths.scattering_types.ScatteringTypes.is_diffuse_on_hole

methods.name: freepaths.scattering_types.ScatteringTypes.is_specular_on_hole
documentation: Was there a specular scattering on holes?

methods.name: freepaths.scattering_types.ScatteringTypes.is_scattered
documentation: Has any of the scattering events occurred?

methods.name: freepaths.scattering_types.ScatteringTypes.reset
documentation: Reset all scattering types to None
Phonon scattering types

This is the main function, which works under Debye approximation.
It should be used to simulate phonon paths at low temperatures

documentation: Simulate a number of phonons and save data to shared datastructure

This class can simulate a number of phonons and save all their data and then return it all
It is meant to be used as a worker for multiprocessing

documentation: Calculate wavelength of the phonon

methods.name: freepaths.phonon.Phonon.is_in_system
documentation:
Checks if the phonon at this timestep did not reach the cold side.
Depending on where user set cold sides, we check if phonon crossed that line

methods.name: freepaths.phonon.Phonon.assign_frequency
documentation: Assigning frequency with probability according to Planckian distribution

methods.name: freepaths.phonon.Phonon.assign_speed
documentation: Calculate group velocity dw/dk according to the frequency and polarization

methods.name: freepaths.phonon.Phonon.assign_internal_scattering_time
documentation: Determine relaxation time after which this phonon will undergo internal scattering

modules.name: freepaths.materials
documentation: Module that assigns physical properties according to chosen material

classes.name: freepaths.materials.Si

methods.name: freepaths.materials.Si.assign_dispersion
documentation: Assign phonon dispersion

methods.name: freepaths.materials.Si.relaxation_time
documentation: Calculate relaxation time at a given frequency and temperature

methods.name: freepaths.materials.Si.assign_heat_capacity
documentation: Calculate heat capacity [J/kg/K] in 3 - 300K range using the polynomial fits

Physical properties of silicon.
Dispersion - Ref. APL 95 161901 (2009)
Relaxation time - Maire et al, Scientific Reports 7, 41794 (2017)
Heat capacity - Desai P.D. Journal of Physical and Chemical Reference Data 15, 67 (1986)

classes.name: freepaths.materials.SiC

methods.name: freepaths.materials.SiC.assign_dispersion
documentation: Assign phonon dispersion

methods.name: freepaths.materials.SiC.relaxation_time
documentation: Calculate relaxation time at a given frequency and temperature including 4 phonon scattering

methods.name: freepaths.materials.SiC.assign_heat_capacity
documentation: Calculate heat capacity [J/kg/K] in 3 - 500K range using the polynomial fits of experimental data

Physical properties of silicon carbide
Dispersion - PRB 50 17054 (1994)
Relaxation time - Joshi et al, JAP 88, 265 (2000)
Heat capacity - Collins et al. Journal of Applied Physics 68, 6510 (1990)

classes.name: freepaths.materials.Graphite

methods.name: freepaths.materials.Graphite.assign_dispersion
documentation: Assign phonon dispersion

methods.name: freepaths.materials.Graphite.relaxation_time
documentation:
Calculate relaxation time at a given frequency and temperature.
In graphite, we assume that material is perfect, i.e. without impurity scattering.

methods.name: freepaths.materials.Graphite.assign_heat_capacity
documentation: Calculate heat capacity [J/kg/K] from the equation

Physical properties of graphite.
Dispersion - Carbon 91 266-274 (2015)
Relaxation time - Ref. PRB 87, 115421 (2013)
Heat capacity - Isaacs, L.L.; Wang, W.Y., Therm. Conduct. 17th, 55-61 (1981)

classes.name: freepaths.materials.Diamond

methods.name: freepaths.materials.Diamond.assign_dispersion
documentation: Assign phonon dispersion

Physical properties of diamond
Dispersion - Ref. PRB 58 12899 (1998)
Materials below are not fully supported and don't have the relaxation times:

classes.name: freepaths.materials.AlN

methods.name: freepaths.materials.AlN.assign_dispersion
documentation: Assign phonon dispersion

Physical properties of AlN.
Dispersion - Yanagisawa et al, Surface and Interface Analysis 37 133-136 (2005)

modules.name: freepaths.holes
documentation:
Module that contains the Hole classes.
These classes contain all methods associated with scattering on holes.

methods.name: freepaths.holes.Hole.is_inside
documentation:
Check if phonon with given coordinates traverses the boundary.
It returns True or False depending whether x, y, z is inside the hole

methods.name: freepaths.holes.Hole.scatter
documentation:
Calculate the new direction after scattering on the hole.
It returns ScatteringTypes object with the scattering type that occued

methods.name: freepaths.holes.Hole.get_patch
documentation:
Create a patch in the shape of the hole to use in the plots.
It returns the matplotlib.patches objects like Rectangle etc.

classes.name: freepaths.holes.CircularHole

methods.name: freepaths.holes.CircularHole.is_inside
documentation: Check if phonon with given coordinates traverses the boundary

methods.name: freepaths.holes.CircularHole.scatter
documentation: Calculate the new direction after scattering on the hole

methods.name: freepaths.holes.CircularHole.get_patch
documentation: Create a patch in the shape of the hole to use in the plots
Shape of a circular hole

classes.name: freepaths.holes.RectangularHole

methods.name: freepaths.holes.RectangularHole.is_inside
documentation: Check if phonon with given coordinates traverses the boundary. It also depens on in the hole is complete or partial.

methods.name: freepaths.holes.RectangularHole.scatter
documentation: Calculate the new direction after scattering on the hole

methods.name: freepaths.holes.RectangularHole.get_patch
documentation: Create a patch in the shape of the hole to use in the plots
Shape of a rectangular hole

modules.name: freepaths.config
documentation:
Module that reads the user input file, provides default values,
checks the validity of the parameters and converts the variables into enums

classes.name: freepaths.config.Config

methods.name: freepaths.config.Config.__init__
documentation: Initiate all the parameters from global variables

methods.name: freepaths.config.Config.convert_to_enums
documentation: Convert some user generated parameters into enums

methods.name: freepaths.config.Config.check_parameter_validity
documentation: Check if various parameters are valid

methods.name: freepaths.config.Config.check_depricated_parameters
documentation: Check for deprecated parameters and warn about them
Class that contains all the settings for the simulation

modules.name: freepaths.run_phonon
documentation:
Module that runs one phonon through the structure, from phonon source to a cold side.
After the run, we record various parameters of this run into flight object.

functions.name: freepaths.run_phonon.run_phonon
documentation: Run one phonon through the system and record parameters of this run

modules.name: freepaths.scattering_semicircle
documentation:
Module that provides scattering functions on parabolic semicircular holes.
The code in this module is contributed by Felix Barbier in 2023.
